task: Allow the published GA4 Google tag to send its collection request through the ResearchOps Pages Content Security Policy.
selectionNote: The model selected GOV.UK and Mural from generic content/tag wording; no GOV.UK or Mural implementation surface is changed.
precedence: GitHub repository controls govern branch and PR handling; ResearchOps and multi-functional controls require a narrow, privacy-aware change; Cloudflare controls apply to the Pages Worker CSP.
evidence: The live GTM Version 2 contains Google tag – ResearchOps GA4 destination with Tag ID G-D446CZRCDD and Initialization - All Pages.
A fresh browser loaded gtag.js for G-D446CZRCDD, then CSP blocked region1.google-analytics.com/g/collect under connect-src.
Google's Tag Platform CSP guidance requires GA4 collection support for https://*.google-analytics.com and https://*.analytics.google.com in connect-src, and https://*.google-analytics.com in img-src.

validation.result: passed (362 tests; rerun after Codex review fix)

residualRisk: harm-register.yaml records that non-essential GTM analytics requires privacy, consent, data-flow and opt-out escalation before enabling tags beyond the approved configuration. This CSP repair does not resolve that governance decision.
